english: Linguine with Crab in Creamy Tomato Sauce

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific entry for linguine al granchio; closest parent 'pasta dish' (FOODON_00002473) used as anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for crab linguine; A00LC (Pasta, cooked) used as closest parent. Crab and sauce components are not separately coded here.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Italian pasta dish with crab.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of linguine al granchio is approximately 350 g, comprising a generous portion of linguine pasta topped with crab meat in creamy tomato sauce.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pasta, cooked, enriched, without added salt (FDC ID 20420) and Crab, blue, cooked, moist heat (FDC ID 15139), combined with cream tomato sauce estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for linguine al granchio as a composite dish. Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked enriched pasta (~55% by weight), cooked blue crab meat (~20% by weight), and a creamy tomato sauce component (~25% by weight, estimated from USDA tomato sauce and cream entries). Weighted average applied per 100 g of finished dish. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Pasta, linguine, durum wheat, boiled
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 46 is based on linguine (durum wheat, boiled) from the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2008 Atkinson et al. international GI tables. The crab meat and creamy tomato sauce components contribute negligible additional glycemic load. GI of the composite dish is estimated to remain in the low range (GI ~46) consistent with the pasta base. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (46 × 61.25) / 100 = 28.2, rounded to 28.3.

hypertension.guidance: A 350 g restaurant serving of linguine al granchio provides approximately 1085 mg sodium, representing nearly half the recommended daily limit of 2300 mg; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-salt preparation and limit portion size.

type2_diabetes.guidance: Despite a low GI of 46, a full restaurant serving delivers approximately 61 g of carbohydrate with a glycemic load of ~28, which is high; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider a half-portion and pair with additional non-starchy vegetables to moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and sodium from cream sauce
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The creamy tomato sauce contributes saturated fat (~6.7 g per serving) and elevated sodium; individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose a lighter tomato-based preparation and limit portion frequency to reduce saturated fat and sodium intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Linguine al Granchio is a classic Italian pasta dish consisting of linguine — long, flat durum wheat pasta — served with crab meat in a creamy tomato sauce. The dish is a staple of Italian coastal cuisine, particularly associated with regions such as Liguria and Campania where fresh crab is abundant. In Singapore, it is served at Italian restaurants such as Ricciotti at The Riverwalk, typically as a premium pasta course priced around SGD 30. The preparation involves boiling linguine to al dente texture, then sautéing crab meat with garlic, white wine, and a blend of tomato sauce and cream to produce a rich, slightly tangy, and savoury sauce. The finished dish has a silky, coating texture with a sweet-savoury flavour profile from the crab and a mild acidity from the tomato. Nutritionally, a restaurant serving (~350 g) provides approximately 518 kcal, 32 g protein, 17 g fat, and 61 g carbohydrate. The dish is a moderate source of vitamin B12 from crab meat (~9.8 mcg per serving), supporting neurological health. Sodium content is elevated (~1085 mg per serving) due to the sauce and crab, making portion control important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index of the pasta base is low (~46), but the glycemic load per full serving is high (~28), requiring carbohydrate awareness for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish contains gluten (pasta) and crustacean allergens (crab) and is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with shellfish allergies.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for linguine al granchio as a composite restaurant dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked pasta, cooked crab meat, and creamy tomato sauce components using weighted proportions. GI is based on the analogous linguine (durum wheat, boiled) entry from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation and restaurant-portion variability.
notes: Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: cooked enriched pasta (FDC 20420), cooked blue crab meat (FDC 15139), and estimated creamy tomato sauce. Weighted proportions: pasta ~55%, crab ~20%, sauce ~25% by finished dish weight. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for the primary components (pasta, crab, cream, tomato sauce) used in this composite dish. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for the pasta and crab components; trace amounts in cream are below detection threshold — confirmed zero). GI of 46 is based on linguine durum wheat boiled from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008). Glycemic load per serving = (46 × 61.25) / 100 = 28.2 (rounded to 28.3). Restaurant serving size of 350 g is an estimate based on typical Italian restaurant pasta portion norms; actual serving weight at Ricciotti may vary. Sodium estimate includes contribution from crab meat, pasta cooking water, and sauce; actual restaurant sodium may be higher. The cuisine_tags field in the input included 'Pizza' and 'Chicken Dish' which are not applicable to this record and have been disregarded. Halal status is not confirmed for this restaurant dish.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full composite nutrient profile from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy weighted components.

Iteration 2: Addressed g3_numeric_hygiene failure by explicitly documenting the reason and source for vitamin_d_mcg = -1 in the 'notes' field, specifying that USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for the primary components (pasta, crab, cream, tomato sauce) do not report it. Also re-verified GI threshold rule, aliases consistency, trans_fat_g justification, ontology match_type values, multilingual rule, and Type 2 Diabetes human_condition_name spelling.
